vulnerabilities.title: Auth Token File Path Disclosure in Error Messages
vulnerabilities.description: Error handlers may expose the full auth file path (~/.zana/auth.json) in stack traces or error logs, revealing the authentication storage location to potential attackers.
vulnerabilities.impact: Attackers knowing the exact location of auth storage could target that file for extraction if filesystem permissions are compromised.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Sanitize error messages to avoid exposing full file paths. Use relative paths or generic messages in user-facing errors.

vulnerabilities.title: Incomplete Environment Variable Sanitization
vulnerabilities.description: Environment variable whitelist filters common vars (PATH, HOME, NODE_*) but may not catch all potentially sensitive environment data. Custom env vars from the host could leak into spawned processes.
vulnerabilities.impact: Sensitive data from the parent process environment could be inherited by child processes, potentially exposing API keys or credentials.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Implement an explicit deny-list of sensitive env vars (AWS_*, GITHUB_*, SECRET_*, *_TOKEN, *_KEY) and remove them from child process environment. Consider using a minimal environment sandbox.

vulnerabilities.title: Token Expiration/Rotation Not Implemented
vulnerabilities.description: Bearer tokens generated with crypto.randomBytes(32) appear to have no expiration mechanism. Tokens issued are perpetual until explicitly revoked.
vulnerabilities.impact: Compromised tokens remain valid indefinitely, increasing the window for unauthorized access. Long-lived tokens are a security anti-pattern.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Implement token expiration (e.g., 24-hour TTL) with refresh token mechanism. Store token issue time and validate expiration on each request.

vulnerabilities.title: CORS Origin Validation Could Be Stricter
vulnerabilities.description: CORS validation checks origin against whitelist, but implementation details suggest it may accept localhost:* or similar patterns that could be overly permissive during development.
vulnerabilities.impact: Overly permissive CORS settings could allow unexpected cross-origin requests in production if configuration isn't properly tightened.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Enforce exact origin matching in production. Use environment-specific CORS config (strict in prod, relaxed in dev). Document CORS exceptions.

vulnerabilities.title: Plugin Loading Without Integrity Verification
vulnerabilities.description: Plugin management system loads external code but no integrity verification (signatures, checksums) is mentioned. Plugins could be tampered with.
vulnerabilities.impact: Malicious or modified plugins could execute arbitrary code with the privileges of the daemon process.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Implement cryptographic signature verification for plugins. Maintain a whitelist of trusted plugin sources. Consider sandboxing plugin execution.

vulnerabilities.title: Control Character Filtering May Not Catch All Injection Vectors
vulnerabilities.description: Sanitization removes control characters but may not catch Unicode-based injection or other encoded payloads.
vulnerabilities.impact: Sophisticated input encoding could potentially bypass argument sanitization in edge cases.
vulnerabilities.recommendation: Use a whitelist approach for allowed characters rather than blacklist. Validate against strict character sets (alphanumeric, underscore, hyphen only for most fields).

positiveFindings: ✓ Command injection prevention: Uses execFileSync with shell:false
✓ Timing-safe comparison: Uses crypto.timingSafeEqual for token validation
✓ File permissions: Auth storage uses 0o600 (owner read/write only)
✓ Bearer token generation: Uses crypto.randomBytes(32) for sufficient entropy
✓ JSON parsing safety: Error handling on JSON.parse prevents parser attacks
✓ CORS validation: Implements origin whitelist checking
✓ No apparent SQL injection: Code structure suggests parameterized queries

recommendations.title: Implement Token Expiration and Rotation
recommendations.description: Add TTL to all bearer tokens (recommended 24 hours) with refresh token mechanism. This significantly reduces the impact of token compromise.

recommendations.title: Strict Environment Variable Sandbox for Child Processes
recommendations.description: Replace whitelist approach with explicit deny-list of sensitive vars. Create minimal environment sandbox with only essential vars (PATH, HOME, minimal NODE vars).

recommendations.title: Plugin Integrity Verification System
recommendations.description: Implement cryptographic signature verification for all plugins. Maintain a trusted plugin registry with checksums.

recommendations.title: Whitelist-Based Input Validation
recommendations.description: Replace character blacklist filtering with strict whitelist validation. Define allowed character sets per input type.

recommendations.title: Production vs Development CORS Configuration
recommendations.description: Implement environment-specific CORS rules. Strict exact-origin matching in production, more relaxed rules only in development.

recommendations.title: Error Message Sanitization
recommendations.description: Audit all error handlers to remove file paths and sensitive system information from user-facing messages.

findings.conclusionAndNextSteps: The Zana codebase demonstrates solid security fundamentals with proper command injection prevention, safe token generation, and appropriate file permissions. The medium risk score (42/100) reflects good baseline security with incremental improvements needed in token lifecycle management and process sandboxing. No critical vulnerabilities were identified. Implementing the HIGH priority recommendations would reduce risk score to approximately 25-30.
